label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. I need to figure out which of the given options is true based on the passage. Let me start by reading the passage again carefully. The first part talks about computers in general: they can solve difficult problems and control machines. People use calculators (small computers) for simple math, scientists use large computers to solve multiple problems at once. You can play games and some computers help learn languages. Then there's the Kurzweil Reading Machine, a special computer that reads books for blind people. The next paragraph explains how to use the Kurzweil machine: a blind person opens the book, puts it on the scanner. The machine reads the words. When the page is done, they turn the page and put it back. The machine can read in normal or fast voice. It can read words with small and large letters. Blind people like it because they can read anything—newspapers, magazines, books, letters. Many libraries have these machines. It can also help short-sighted children learn to read. Now, the question is which statement is true. Let's look at the options: A) Computers can solve all the problems people can't do. The passage says computers can solve difficult problems, but "all the problems people can't do" is too strong. The passage doesn't say they can solve all unsolvable problems. So probably A is false. B) Large computers can only solve one problem at a time. No, the passage states that scientists use large computers to solve many problems at the same time. So B is the opposite of what's stated. False. C) When using the Kurzweil machine, a blind man needn't do anything. The passage says the blind person has to open the book, put it on the scanner, turn the page
